query: 2.0: 1.6T MoE trained entirely on AI ASICs review

results.title: LongCat-2.0 - Frontier reasoning models from Meituan
results.content:
Launching today

1.6T MoE trained entirely on AI ASICs

LongCat-2.0 is an MIT-licensed 1.6T-parameter MoE model with ~48B active parameters, 1M context, LongCat Sparse Attention, and post-training for coding and agentic workflows. It was trained on AI ASIC superpods and integrates with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes.

Foundation Models

LongCat is the AI model series from Meituan, featuring powerful, efficient, and open-source models for complex tasks. Its latest release, LongCat-Flash-Thinking, is a 560B parameter MoE reasoning model that sets a new standard for speed and performance.

## LongCat-2.0

Launching today

1.6T MoE trained entirely on AI ASICs [...] Promoted

Zac Zuo

Flowtica Scribe

Flowtica Scribe

📌

Hi everyone!  
  
LongCat-2.0 is a 1.6T-parameter MoE model with about 48B active parameters per token, 1M context, and open weights under MIT.  
  
But it was not trained in the usual Nvidia-heavy way. The full training run was built on AI ASIC superpods, over more than 35T tokens, with no rollback or irrecoverable loss spike.  
  
Training a trillion-scale model is already hard. Getting that run stable on alternative hardware is probably the more interesting story here 🤔

Madalina B

awww, cuteeee

DeepSeek gallery image
DeepSeek gallery image
Marked 3 [...] I use different AI tools for different tasks, but DeepSeek stands out because of its strong reasoning, coding ability, and accessibility. It is especially useful when I want a low-cost or free AI model that can still handle complex technical work.

Compared with some alternatives, DeepSeek feels like a strong option for developers who care about reasoning depth, coding help, and affordability.

Qiuyu
Jaouane Brahim

#### What's great [...] I use it for coding help, debugging, technical explanations, research, writing, and breaking down complex problems. The deep thinking ability is especially useful when I need step-by-step reasoning for architecture decisions, algorithm ideas, or technical troubleshooting.

What I like most is that DeepSeek can handle complex prompts without feeling too limited, and it gives high-quality responses for both development and research tasks. The free tier and open-source direction also make it very attractive for developers, students, and builders who want powerful AI without heavy subscription costs.

#### What needs improvement

DeepSeek is powerful, but the biggest improvement areas are reliability and speed.
